meaning: Including or considering all elements or aspects; fully comprehensive; thorough. | Including or considering all elements or aspects; fully comprehensive.
example: The researchers conducted an exhaustive study of the data.
example2: She gave an exhaustive list of every possible error.
example3: The detective made an exhaustive search of the premises.
Explanation: Exhaustive is an adjective that describes something done with extreme thoroughness, leaving nothing out. It implies great effort and attention to detail to cover every part of a subject. It is stronger than 'complete'; a complete list has all items, but an exhaustive list has been compiled after an immense effort to ensure nothing is missed. A common mistake is confusing it with 'exhausting' (tiring); 'exhaustive' refers to thoroughness, not fatigue, though exhaustive work can be exhausting. This word is a high compliment in research, analysis, and investigation, indicating maximum possible coverage.
usageTip: Use 'exhaustive' to praise the extreme thoroughness and completeness of a piece of work or search.

meaning: Including all possibilities, details, or aspects; very thorough
example: The research team conducted an exhaustive study on the new virus.
example2: They made an exhaustive search for the missing files.
example3: The manual provides an exhaustive list of all configuration options.
Explanation: The word 'exhaustive' describes something that covers every possible part or detail. It is commonly used in professional contexts to indicate that no stone has been left unturned during an investigation or review. Unlike 'exhausting', which refers to something that makes you tired, 'exhaustive' relates to completeness. It is important to use this term when discussing thorough research or comprehensive lists. It ensures that the audience knows nothing has been missed.
usageTip: Use 'exhaustive' when you mean that something is as complete as possible.
